reason:
PROPERNESS PROOF GAP -- SURJECTIVITY OF gamma^0 NOT ESTABLISHED: Step 1 proves gamma^0: R->R is a diffeomorphism from R onto its image f(R). Step 2(i) claims f^{-1}(pi_0(K)) is a compact interval, but f^{-1} is only defined on f(R), not all of R. If f(R) is a proper subinterval of R, then pi_0(K) may extend beyond f(R), and f^{-1}(pi_0(K) cap f(R)) can equal all of R (unbounded).

COUNTEREXAMPLE: gamma(tau)=(arctan(tau),0,0,0) satisfies ALL hypotheses of Definition 1: smooth embedding, timelike (eta=-1/(1+tau^2)^2<0), future-directed (1/(1+tau^2)>0), I=R. But f(R)=(-pi/2,pi/2). For K=[-1,1]^4, gamma^{-1}(K)=R (not compact). gamma is NOT proper.

The fix is to add the normalization eta_{ab} dot-gamma^a dot-gamma^b = -1 (proper-time parametrisation). With this, (dot-gamma^0)^2 = 1 + sum(dot-gamma^i)^2 >= 1, so dot-gamma^0 >= 1 (using future-directedness), hence gamma^0(tau) >= tau + const -> +inf, giving surjectivity. Alternatively, add the explicit hypothesis gamma^0(tau)->pm infty as tau->pm infty. The current definition allows timelike embeddings that are not proper.
